import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";
import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Get signature qualities details for a user"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"get"}
  path={"/user/signature-qualities"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



The result of this query will tell you what final signature `quality` will be
used for the given user `username` to sign a document.  

The resulting signature quality is determined in combination of the
requested `quality` and `legislation`, whereas any stands for any legislation.  

Description for the example request:  

If you create a `SignatureRequest` with `quality=AES_MINIMAL` and the
`legislation=ZERTES` the user in this example would finally sign the document
with the signature quality `QES`.  

As described in the previous chapter `AES_MINIMAL` will use `QES` as long as the
user settings allow this.

  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
